content.content: {"body":"* **Suggested term label:** Sclerotizing precursor biosynthetic process * * **ChEBI ID and label of the substance:** CHEBI:192799 - β-alanyl-dopaminium(1+) CHEBI:125678 - N-acetyldopamine For the other metabolites I couldn’t find CHEBI IDs * **Definition (free text)** The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of N-beta-alanyldopamine (NBAD) and N-acetyldopamine (NADA) and their ortho- and para-quinones and hydro- derivatives. In insects, these sclerotizing precursors are involved in the cross-linking of cuticular proteins hardening the cuticle (sclerotisation), and in cuticle pigmentation. * **Reference, in format PMID:#######** PMID:32709620 * **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term** Drosophila genes: e speck stw * **Parent term(s)** GO:0046148 pigment biosynthetic process * **Synonym(s) (please specify, EXACT, BROAD, NARROW or RELATED)** N-beta-alanyldopamine (NBAD) and N-acetyldopamine (NADA) biosynthesis (narrow match) * **Cross-references** No cross-references * **Any other information** Taxon constrain for insects ---- * **FOR ONTOLOGY EDITOR** * **Logical definition** * * **substance metabolic process:** 'metabolic process' and ('has primary input or output' some [substance] * * **substance biosynthetic process:** 'biosynthetic process' and ('has primary output' some [substance]. * * **substance catabolic process:** 'catabolic process' and ('has primary output' some [substance] * **Standard synonyms** * * **substance metabolic process:** * * * EXACT: substance metabolism * * **substance biosynthetic process:** * * * EXACT: substance anabolism * * * EXACT: substance biosynthesis * * * EXACT: substance formation * * * EXACT: substance synthesis * * **substance catabolic process:** * * * EXACT: substance breakdown * * * EXACT: substance catabolism * * * EXACT: substance degradation ","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBQPmxQ","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@rozaru We cannot do an 'and'. We can either create two terms, or use a more general ChEBI parent. In insects, these sclerotizing precursors are involved in the cross-linking of cuticular proteins hardening the cuticle (sclerotisation), and in cuticle pigmentation. Reference: PMID:32709620 PMID: 19932179 Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term Drosophila genes: e stw Parent term(s) GO:0046148 pigment biosynthetic process Synonym(s) (please specify, EXACT, BROAD, NARROW or RELATED) N-beta-alanyldopamine (NBAD) biosynthesis (narrow match) -- **NADA-derived sclerotizing precursor biosynthetic process** CHEBI:125678 - N-acetyldopamine Definition The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of N-acetyldopamine (NADA) and its ortho- and para-quinones and hydro- derivatives. In insects, these sclerotizing precursors are involved in the cross-linking of cuticular proteins hardening the cuticle (sclerotisation), and in cuticle pigmentation. Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2026-05-06T06:09:53Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32029#issuecomment-4385546519","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBWuCqA","author":{"login":"rozaru"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"It would be better but I wanted to have a GO term that can cover the whole biosynthesis of sclerotizing precursors and not just beta-alanyl-dopamine and N-acetyldopamine. Could beta-alanyl-dopamine and derivatives biosynthetic process works (it's not great) but it would match the definition. Below is how the biosynthesis apthway looks like: ","createdAt":"2026-05-06T07:19:18Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32029#issuecomment-4385899176","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBXRznA","author":{"login":"sjm41"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Hi @rozaru From your diagram, it looks like we'd want two terms to cover the two pathways from start to end, like this: dehydro-NBAD biosynthetic process (L-tyrosine -> dehydro-NBAD) dehydro-NADA biosynthetic process (L-tyrosine -> dehydro-NADA) Or maybe you're saying that the pathway doesn't always go through to the final product shown on your diagram? This 2022 review: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/chapter/bookseries/pii/S0065280622000017 has sections titled: "22. Dehydro NBAD formation" "14. Biosynthesis of dehydro NADA" ","createdAt":"2026-05-06T08:58:48Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32029#issuecomment-4386485148","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBfIg3w","author":{"login":"rozaru"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> Or maybe you're saying that the pathway doesn't always go through to the final product shown on your diagram? Yes, the intermediates can form adducts. Having said that your proposal would work well as it would cover all of them. I couldn’t find a ChEBI ID for the dehydro version so if the following terms are created I will ask ChEBI to curate them so we can have an ID: 1,2-dehydro-N-β-alanyldopamine biosynthetic process 1,2-dehydro-N-acetyldopamine biosynthetic process","createdAt":"2026-05-07T06:42:36Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32029#issuecomment-4394721503","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBfyM2w","author":{"login":"sjm41"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I've made the two new terms as shown below - let me know if any changes are needed. @rozaru Please update this ticket when ChEBI has made the new terms - then I can add them as the LD. @pgaudet Can you take care of the taxon constraints - both terms should be 'only in insects'. [Term] id: GO:0120576 name: 1,2-dehydro-N-beta-alanyldopamine biosynthetic process namespace: biological_process def: "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of 1,2-dehydro-N-beta-alanyldopamine via N-beta-alanyldopamine (NBAD). In insects, these sclerotizing precursors are involved in the cross-linking of cuticular proteins hardening (sclerotizing) and pigmenting the cuticle." content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /__w/go-ontology/go-ontology/.claude/skills/taxon-constraint

These live in `src/taxon_constraints`

- `never_in_taxon.tsv`
- `only_in_taxon.tsv`

Taxon constraints don't get added in the main go-edit.obo file.

## Creating new taxon constraints

These should generally be done under instruction. You should balance following user instructions with parsimony. Sometimes it is better to:

- make never_in_taxon constraints to a more general clade than requested
- make only_in_taxon constraints to a more general clade than requested

However, great care can be taken. Don't assume TCs based just on the GO name. Sometimes GO will use terms like "heart development" in a very general way (encompassing insect 'hearts'), and someimes in a more specific way.

Remember, if a term references an external ontology like CL and UBERON, it's likely these ontologies will have the appropriate TC, which is inherted. No need for duplication.

## Querying NCBITaxon

Use OAK with the OLS adapter:

`runoak -i ols:ncbitaxon info Mammalia`

## Evidence

Although there is no place to put evidence for TCs, you should do due diligence and research - both phylogenetic, and on the usage of the GO term. Report this when you communicate back to the issue/PR using `gh`.

## Taxon constraints and obsoletion

When obsoleting a term, you may need to remove the taxon constraint, as these should never point to obsoletes

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0120576
name: 1,2-dehydro-N-beta-alanyldopamine biosynthetic process
namespace: biological_process
def: "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of 1,2-dehydro-N-beta-alanyldopamine via N-beta-alanyldopamine (NBAD). In insects, these sclerotizing precursors are involved in the cross-linking of cuticular proteins hardening (sclerotizing) and pigmenting the cuticle." [PMID:19932179, PMID:32709620]
synonym: "N-beta-alanyldopamine biosynthesis" NARROW []
synonym: "NBAD biosynthesis" NARROW []
is_a: GO:0046148 ! pigment biosynthetic process
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32029" xsd:anyURI
created_by: sjm
creation_date: 2026-05-07T08:02:35Z
